hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific TM5/ECL2 interactions, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will improve steric complementarity in the SSTR2-specific ECL3 pocket, thereby increasing the selectivity margin over off-target subtypes.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: To achieve SSTR2 selectivity, we must focus on disrupting off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions 1, 2, 5, 6, 11, and 12. Strong ddG alone is insufficient if selectivity is not achieved.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are known to contribute to off-target interactions with SSTR3 and SSTR5. Focusing mutations here can disrupt conserved-pocket contacts without affecting the pharmacophore (positions 7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).
These mutations are selected to disrupt conserved interactions with SSTR3 and SSTR5 while maintaining flexibility and avoiding steric clashes.
